title: Dashboard history JSON schema guide
intro: Use this help surface when CI, scripts, or external tooling need to understand the machine-readable documents emitted by `dashboard history`.
generalRule: Branch on `kind` plus `schemaVersion` before reading nested fields.
`dashboard history list` and `dashboard history restore` switch machine-readable output with `--output-format json`.
`dashboard history diff` compares two historical versions from live Grafana or local history artifacts.
`dashboard history export` always writes a JSON artifact through `--output`.
`dashboard history restore` does not rewind Grafana history in place; it copies one historical version forward as a new latest revision entry.

title: Dashboard history list JSON schema
commands: grafana-util dashboard history list --url http://localhost:3000 --token "$GRAFANA_API_TOKEN" --dashboard-uid cpu-main --output-format json

contractSections.label: grafana-util-dashboard-history-list
contractSections.description: returned for one dashboard history stream from live Grafana or one local artifact

contractSections.label: grafana-util-dashboard-history-inventory
contractSections.description: returned when `--input-dir` is used without `--dashboard-uid`, so you can inventory available history artifacts first

title: Dashboard history restore JSON schema
commands: grafana-util dashboard history restore --url http://localhost:3000 --basic-user admin --basic-password admin --dashboard-uid cpu-main --version 17 --dry-run --output-format json
notes: `--dry-run` renders the same contract without mutating Grafana.
A live restore still creates a new latest revision entry; it does not remove the historical version you selected.
Grafana may keep the same numeric `version` in some environments, so treat the new history entry and message as the reliable signal.

title: Dashboard history diff JSON schema
commands: grafana-util dashboard history diff --url http://localhost:3000 --basic-user admin --basic-password admin --base-dashboard-uid cpu-main --base-version 17 --new-dashboard-uid cpu-main --new-version 21 --output-format text

grafana-util dashboard history diff --base-input-dir ./exports-2026-04-01 --base-dashboard-uid cpu-main --base-version 17 --new-input-dir ./exports-2026-04-07 --new-dashboard-uid cpu-main --new-version 21 --output-format json
generalRule: Branch on `kind` plus `schemaVersion` before reading nested fields.
Each side is resolved independently from live Grafana, one reusable history artifact, or one export root created with `dashboard export --include-history`.
notes: Use the `base` and `new` flags symmetrically so the compare source is explicit.
Two dated export roots for the same dashboard UID can be compared directly.

title: Dashboard history export JSON schema
commands: grafana-util dashboard history export --url http://localhost:3000 --token "$GRAFANA_API_TOKEN" --dashboard-uid cpu-main --output ./cpu-main.history.json
notes: This command always writes the JSON contract to `--output`.
Use `--overwrite` when you intentionally want to replace an existing artifact file.
